name: Node.js Module._load() policy bypass
description: The use of Module._load() can bypass the policy mechanism and require modules outside of the policy.json definition.

name: Node.js Permissions policy bypass via deprecated API
description: A privilege escalation vulnerability exists in the experimental Permission model when the --allow-fs-read flag is used with a non-* argument.

name: HTTP chunk extension DoS
description: A vulnerability in the HTTP parser allows a denial of service attack via a specially crafted chunked transfer encoding with excessive chunk extensions.

name: HTTP/2 Rapid Reset DDoS
description: The HTTP/2 protocol allows denial of service via rapid stream creation and immediate RST_STREAM. Affects Node.js HTTP/2 server.

name: HTTP smuggling via llhttp Transfer-Encoding
description: The llhttp parser in Node.js does not properly handle multi-line Transfer-Encoding headers, enabling HTTP request smuggling.

name: HTTP smuggling via multi-line Transfer-Encoding
description: The llhttp parser incorrectly handles multi-line Transfer-Encoding headers, which can be exploited for HTTP request smuggling attacks.

name: Use-after-free in TLS implementation
description: Node.js has a use-after-free bug in the TLS implementation when handling certain sequences of TLS operations.

name: DNS rebinding via improper hostname validation
description: Node.js does not properly validate the hostname returned by DNS resolvers, allowing DNS rebinding attacks that bypass same-origin restrictions.

name: HTTP Request Smuggling via CR-to-Hyphen
description: HTTP Transfer-Encoding handling allows request smuggling when carriage return is converted to a hyphen, bypassing request validation.

name: napi_get_value_string buffer overflow
description: Multiple napi_get_value_string_* functions can cause buffer overflow when passed a sufficiently large string, leading to potential code execution.

name: Linux CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE privilege escalation
description: On Linux, Node.js ignores certain environment variables when running with elevated capabilities (CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE), enabling privilege escalation.

name: Path traversal via overlong Windows path
description: The Permission model fails to properly validate a path traversal sequence constructed with overlong UTF-8 path segments.

name: HTTP smuggling via malformed Transfer-Encoding
description: HTTP request smuggling using malformed Transfer-Encoding header allows an attacker to desynchronize request processing.

name: HTTP header whitespace handling bypass
description: Including whitespace in HTTP header values allows bypass of security controls that depend on strict header parsing.

name: Express.js qs prototype pollution
description: The qs module used by Express.js before 6.10.3 allows attackers to cause prototype pollution via crafted query string parameters.
recommendation: Upgrade Express.js to 4.17.3+ or qs to 6.10.3+.

name: Express.js Open Redirect
description: Versions of Express.js before 4.19.2 are vulnerable to open redirect when untrusted user input is passed to the response.redirect() function.

name: Express.js XSS via response.redirect()
description: In Express.js, passing untrusted user input to response.redirect() may cause XSS when the URL contains the \n character.

name: Next.js middleware authorization bypass
description: Next.js middleware-based authorization can be bypassed by setting the x-middleware-subrequest header, allowing unauthenticated access to protected routes.

name: Fetch API decompression bomb DoS
description: A vulnerability in the Node.js fetch() API allows an attacker to cause excessive resource consumption by sending a specially crafted compressed response.

name: process.mainModule policy bypass
description: The use of proto with process.mainModule.require() can bypass the experimental policy mechanism.
